comments.body: Good and bad news for you. Good: I got the same problem. Bad: I have no idea why, too.

answers.body:
It seems that you have both "jetty" (org.eclipse.jetty) and "tomcat" (org.apache.tomcat, org.apache.catalina) in your stacktrace so something wierd is going on.

Are you using maven? Try running mvn dependency:tree to see why both tomcat and jetty are on your classpath

comments.body: You could update the question with this info instead of adding it as an answer

answers.body:
I'm actually not using Maven, I would like the service run's with Tomcat 10. But I don't know how to do this.

With Tomcat 7.0.27 it was running all fine. Probably with the new version some changes happened and I don't know where to configure this or what library is missing.

comments.body: Your answer could be improved with additional supporting information. Please edit to add further details, such as citations or documentation, so that others can confirm that your answer is correct. You can find more information on how to write good answers in the help center.

answers.body:
We had the exact same case as you: 1 Java 17 2 Tomcat 10 3 Apache CXF 4.0.2

In our case we imported a library which used jetty for some testing purposes. But we were using tomcat to run our own application. Our solution was excluding the jetty import from this library and it worked like a charm.

I ran into exactly this same issue. In my case, I had the CXF runtime in my Java Build Path in Eclipse. This works just fine for Tomcat 9 and earlier. However, Tomcat 10.1 and TomEE 9 both produce that exception. The fix for me was to remove the CXF runtime from my build path and add the specific dependencies that I need in my POM. The clue that gave it away for me was lance-java's answer.

Both Tomcat and Jetty are servlet containers and there's some conflict happening when Tomcat is trying to initialize an application that is compiled along with the Jetty dependencies.

I've encountered this error while building a Clojure project with Lein, and what helped me was excluding Jetty-related modules from the libraries that use them ("ring" in my case) in the compilation dependencies:

[ring "1.15.3" :exclusions [ring/ring-jetty-adapter]]

If you are using Maven, that would probably look like this:

<dependency>
    <groupId>ring</groupId>
    <artifactId>ring</artifactId>
    <version>1.15.3</version>
    <exclusions>
        <exclusion>
            <groupId>ring</groupId>
            <artifactId>ring-jetty-adapter</artifactId>
        </exclusion>
    </exclusions>
</dependency>

You can run mvn dependency:tree to find which library has Jetty as a transitive dependency.

title: java.lang.IllegalStateException No Jetty ContextHandler, Jetty WebSocket SCI unavailable
body:
I'm trying to deploy a SOAP-Webservice with:

  • Tomcat 10
  • Apache CXF 4.0.2
  • Java JDK 17.0.6

The Service was running fine with Tomcat 7.0.27, Java JDK 1.7.45 and Apache CXF 3.0.2. After changing all versions now it won't run again and claims the above error message. I have no idea, what I can do.

This are the details of the error message.

I tried several changes of the configuration, but nothing helps. I'm working with Eclipse Version: 2023-06 (4.28.0)

Many thanks for any help.
